name: generic_video_provider_capabilities
purpose: Describe video API capabilities without committing a real provider endpoint, model, payload, token, key name, or user-specific request body.

capabilityTiers.useWhen: The provider accepts multiple image or video references.
capabilityTiers.strategy: Send separate character sheets plus the node storyboard or scene image; bind every reference by role in the prompt.

capabilityTiers.useWhen: The provider accepts start and end frames.
capabilityTiers.strategy: Generate consistent first and last frames from the same character anchors; split complex nodes into short clips.

capabilityTiers.useWhen: The provider accepts only one reference image.
capabilityTiers.strategy: Use a no-text composite reference image: left identity strip, right keyframes. Focus each clip on one character or one relationship.

capabilityTiers.useWhen: The provider accepts prompt text only.
capabilityTiers.strategy: Treat character consistency as prototype risk; reduce multi-character close-ups and rely on strong identity anchors.

capabilityTiers.useWhen: The user provides videos or the project uses local static-image synthesis.
capabilityTiers.strategy: Map local videos into assets/videos or mark static synthesis as prototype.

qualityRules: Ask the user to choose or provide the video provider before API generation.
Do not assume any provider supports multiple references, first/last frame, or async polling; inspect the user-provided API format first.
Never paste user keys, tokens, cookies, signed URLs, or account-specific payloads into this repository.
When quota is limited, prefer duration <= 8 seconds and avoid resubmitting successful or running jobs.
If same-face, face-swap, or costume fusion appears, diagnose reference quality, identity anchors, prompt binding, and provider capability before retrying.
